blocks:
Surah At-Takathur

Medinan, and it is said Meccan, eight verses by consensus. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١)** أَلْهاكُمُ التَّكاثُرُ ** (Rivalry in increasing [worldly] possessions distracts you) The boasting of abundance distracts you.

(٢) حَتَّى زُرْتُمُ الْمَقابِرَ ** (until you visit the graves.) Until, when you had encompassed the number of the living, you proceeded to the graves and boasted of the abundance (تَكَاثَرْتُمْ) of the dead. He expressed their transition to mentioning the dead by the phrase "visiting the graves." And it is said its meaning is: أَلْهاكُمُ التَّكاثُرُ ** (Rivalry in increasing [worldly] possessions distracts you) through wealth and children, until you died and were buried, having wasted your lives in the pursuit of this world, neglecting what is more important for you, which is striving for your Hereafter. Thus, visiting the graves (زِيَارَةُ الْقُبُورِ) is a metaphor (كِنَايَة) for death.

In Nahj al-Balagha there is that which supports the first meaning, where he (peace be upon him) said after reciting this Surah: "Do they boast of the resting places of their fathers, or do they boast of the abundance (يَتَكَاثَرُونَ) of the perished? He said: And that they should be lessons (عِبَرًا) is more deserving than that they should be a source of pride (مُفْتَخَرًا). And that they should descend from them to the side of humility (ذِلَّة) is more appropriate than that they should stand by them in a position of glory (عِزَّة)."

In Rawdat al-Wa'izin, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, there is that which indicates the second meaning. He said: He recited أَلْهاكُمُ التَّكاثُرُ (Rivalry in increasing [worldly] possessions distracts you) and said: The boasting of abundance (تَكَاثُر) of wealth is gathering it unjustly and tying it up in containers, **حَتَّى زُرْتُمُ الْمَقابِرَ ** (until you visit the graves), meaning until you enter your graves.

In Al-Majma', narrated from him صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: He recited this Surah and said: The son of Adam says, "My wealth! My wealth!" Yet, what do you possess of your wealth except what you ate and thus consumed, or wore and thus wore out, or gave in charity (تَصَدَّقْتَ) and thus sent forth?

(٣) كَلَّا سَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ. (3) No indeed! Soon you will know. In the previous Hadith al-Rawdah, He (peace be upon him) said: "If you were to enter your graves."

(٤) ثُمَّ كَلَّا سَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ ** (4) Again, no indeed! Soon you will know. He (peace be upon him) said: "If you were to exit your graves toward your place of gathering (محشركم)."

(٥) كَلَّا لَوْ تَعْلَمُونَ عِلْمَ الْيَقِينِ ** (5) No indeed! If you knew with the knowledge of certainty, He (peace be upon him) said: "And that is when the Bridge (الصراط) is brought and erected between the two sides (جسري) of Hell (جهنّم)."

And in al-Mahasin, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), regarding the Almighty's saying: لَوْ تَعْلَمُونَ عِلْمَ الْيَقِينِ ** (If you knew with the knowledge of certainty), He (peace be upon him) said: "It is the direct witnessing (المعاينة)."

(6) لَتَرَوُنَّ الْجَحِيمَ ** (6) You will surely see the Hellfire! And it was recited with the dammah on the tā’ (لَتُرَوُنَّ). Al-Majma' narrated this from Ali (peace be upon him). (7) ** ثُمَّ لَتَرَوُنَّها عَيْنَ الْيَقِينِ ** (7) Again, you will surely see it with the eye of certainty. And perhaps that is when they approach it. (8) ** ثُمَّ لَتُسْئَلُنَّ يَوْمَئِذٍ عَنِ النَّعِيمِ. (8) Then, on that day, you will surely be questioned concerning the bounty. In al-Rawdah, in the preceding narration, He said: Concerning five things: the filling of bellies, cold drink, the pleasure of sleep, the shade of dwellings, and the soundness of creation (اعتدال الخلق). In al-Majma', narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): It is security (الأمن) and health (الصحة). In al-Uyun, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), He said: Fresh dates (الرطب) and cold water. In al-Faqih, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: Every bounty (نعيم) will be questioned about by its possessor, except that which was spent in a raid (غزو) or Hajj (حجّ). In al-Majalis, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: Whoever mentions the Name of Allah over food will not be questioned concerning the bounty (نعيم) of that food. And al-Qummi narrated from Him (peace be upon him), He said: This Ummah will be questioned concerning what Allah bestowed upon them as bounty (أنعم) through the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله and then through His Household (أهل بيته). In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a hadith: The bounty (النعيم) about which the questioning will occur is the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله and those who took his place among the chosen ones (أصفياء) of Allah, for Allah bestowed them as a bounty upon those of His friends (أوليائهم) who followed them. And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Abu Hanifah asked Him about this Ayah. So He said to him: "What is the bounty (النَّعِيمِ) in your view, O Nu'man?" He replied: "Sustenance from food and cold water." He (al-Sadiq) said: "If Allah were to make you stand before Him on the Day of Resurrection until He questions you about every morsel you ate or every drink you drank, your standing before Him would surely be prolonged." So he asked: "Then what is the bounty (النَّعِيمِ), may I be made your ransom (جعلت فداك)?" He replied: "We, the Household (أهل البيت), are the bounty (النعيم) with which Allah favored the servants. Through us, they were united after they had been divided, and through us, Allah reconciled their hearts and made them brothers after they had been enemies. Through us, Allah guided them to Islam, and that is the blessing (النعمة) which is never cut off. And Allah will question them concerning the right of the bounty (حقّ النعيم) with which He favored them, and that is the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and His pure progeny (عترته) عليهم السلام." And in a narration: He (al-Sadiq) said to him: "It has reached me that you interpret the bounty (النعيم) in this Ayah as delicious food and cold water on a hot day." He replied: "Yes." He (al-Sadiq) said: "If a man invited you and fed you delicious food and gave you cold water to drink, and then he held it as a favor over you (امتنّ عليك به), to what would you attribute him?" He replied: "To stinginess (البخل)." He (al-Sadiq) said: "Would Allah, the Exalted, be stingy (أفيبخل الله تعالى)?" He replied: "Then what is it?" He (al-Sadiq) said: "Our love, the love of the Household (أهل البيت)." In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him), He said: "There is no true bounty (نعيم حقيقي) in this world." So, one of the jurists (الفقهاء) who was present said to Him: "Then why does Allah, the Exalted, say: ثُمَّ لَتُسْئَلُنَّ يَوْمَئِذٍ عَنِ النَّعِيمِ ** (Then, on that day, you will surely be questioned concerning the bounty)? Surely, this bounty (النعيم) in the world is cold water." Al-Rida (peace be upon him) replied, raising His voice: "Thus have you interpreted it, and you have made it into various types! One group said it is cold water, others said it is delicious food, and still others said it is pleasant sleep. My father narrated to me from his father, Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him), that these sayings of yours were mentioned in His presence regarding the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: ثُمَّ لَتُسْئَلُنَّ يَوْمَئِذٍ عَنِ النَّعِيمِ ** (Then, on that day, you will surely be questioned concerning the bounty). So He became angry and said: 'Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, does not question His servants concerning what He has bestowed upon them out of His grace, nor does He hold it as a favor over them (يمنّ بذلك عليهم). Holding a favor over someone for a blessing (الامتنان بالانعام) is considered repulsive from created beings, so how can that which created beings dislike be attributed to the Creator, the Mighty and Majestic? Rather, the bounty (النّعيم) is our love, the love of the Household (أهل البيت), and our loyalty (موالاتنا). Allah will question about it after Tawhid (monotheism) and Prophethood, because if the servant fulfills that, it leads him to the bounty (نعيم) of Paradise, which never ceases.'" In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) concerning this Ayah, He said: "Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, is too exalted and generous to feed you food and make it easy for you to swallow (فسوّغكموه) and then question you about it. Rather, He will question you concerning what He bestowed upon you as a bounty (أنعم عليكم) through Muhammad and the Family of Muhammad عليهم السلام." And in a narration from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "He will only question you concerning the truth (الحقّ) upon which you stand." In al-Mahasin, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: "Three things the believing servant will not be held accountable for: food he eats, clothing he wears, and a righteous wife who assists him and through whom he protects his private parts (فرجه)." And in a narration, He said: "Indeed, Allah is too generous to question a believer about his eating and drinking." I say: Perhaps the reconciliation between the narrations is to say that no one will be questioned concerning the necessities of food, clothing, and other essentials. Rather, the questioning will be concerning what exceeded the necessity, and concerning the bounty (أنعم الله به) that Allah bestowed, which is the guidance to the love (مودّة) of the Household (أهل البيت) and obedience to them—how they dealt with them عليهم السلام. In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever recites Surah Alhakum al-Takathur (سورة ألهاكم التكاثر) in an obligatory prayer (فريضة), Allah will record for him the reward (أجر) of one hundred martyrs. And whoever recites it in a supererogatory prayer (نافلة), the reward (أجر) of fifty martyrs will be recorded for him, and forty rows of angels will pray with him in the obligatory prayer.
